compiled_state.title: Agent confusion over review escalation table — contradictory prose added without fixing underlying table
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## What happened

During the fix for bug b5e2-56ad, the orchestrator added prose to REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md stating that tier escalation should dispatch '3 parallel sonnet specialists + opus architectural synthesis' when light/standard tier exhausts attempts. However, the EXISTING escalation table (PASS_NUM 3+ model escalation) maps light/standard to dso:code-reviewer-deep-arch (opus alone), NOT the full 3-sonnet + opus path. The full deep-tier path is only activated for already-deep-tier re-reviews (RE_REVIEW_DEEP_FULL=true).

The standard-tier reviewer correctly identified this contradiction. The orchestrator dismissed the finding, citing user feedback memory that says 'escalate to full deep tier.' But the fix should have ALSO updated the escalation table to match the new prose — adding contradictory prose without fixing the table makes the workflow internally inconsistent.

## Fix needed

1. Clarify the escalation table in REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md so it is unambiguous about what gets dispatched when light/standard tier exhausts attempts
2. Consider restructuring the escalation table and prose so agents cannot be confused about which path to follow
3. Ensure the cached summary in sprint SKILL.md is consistent with the resolved table
